description: Title: Islamic fascism Author: Abdel-Samad, Hamed, 1972- author. ◄☀►[ Dewey Class Number := 320. 53309 ABD ] ◄☀► ☀► Subject Term ◄☀ Fascism -- Arab countries National socialism and Islam. Political culture -- Arab countries. ================================= ISBN:  9781633881242 Publication Information: Amherst, New York :  Prometheus Books, 2016. Publisher: Prometheus Books, Physical Description: 255 pages ; 24 cm ☀► Language Note: Translated from German. ◄☀ ================================= ================================= ☀► Table of Contents ◄☀ Introduction: Wanted Dead p. 9 Chapter 1 An Odd Couple? Fascism and Islamism in Recent History p. 15 Chapter 2 Reformists or Fascist Islamists? The Muslim Brotherhood in Egypt p. 23 Chapter 3 Islamic Fascism's Historic Roots from Abraham to Sayyid Qutb p. 47 Chapter 4 From My Struggle (Mein Kampf) to Our Struggle- Arabia and Anti-Semitism p. 65 Chapter 5 From Gutenberg to Zuckerberg-Cultural Monopoly and the Dictatorship of Islam p. 83 Chapter 6 "Heil Osama!"-Failed States and Successful Terrorists p. 99 Chapter 7 Pornotopia-Jihad and the Promise of Paradise p. 105 Chapter 8 Islamic Bombs and Shiite Fascism p. 115 Chapter 9 Unbelievers on the March-Meet Five Atheists from the Islamic World p. 135 Chapter 10 Salafists, Jihadists, and Islamic Fascism in Europe p. 151 Chapter 11 Polarization and Social Cleansing-What Thilo Sarrazin and Recep Tayyip Erdogan Have in Common p. 169 Chapter 12 Mapping the Terrain of Terror-Islamism, Islam, and the Islamic State p. 177 Chapter 13 Charlie Hebdo and Islam's Outrage Industry p. 193 Afterword: Islamism and the Endgame p. 207 Acknowledgments p. 217 Notes p. 219 Bibliography p. 231 Index p. 239 About the Author p. 255 ================================= ================================= ☀► Abstract ◄☀ "This polemic against Islamic extremism highlights  the striking parallels between contemporary Islamism and  the 20th-century fascism embodied by Hitler and Mussolini.  Like those infamous ideologies,  Islamism today touts imperialist dreams of world domination,  belief in its inherent superiority, contempt for the rest of humanity,  and often a murderous agenda.  The author, born and raised in Egypt and now living in Germany,  not only explains the historical connections between early 20th-century fascist movements in Europe and extremist factions in Islam,  but he also traces the fascist tendencies in mainstream Islam that have existed throughout its history.  Examining key individuals and episodes from centuries past, the book shows the influence of Islam's earliest exploits on current politics in the Islamic world.  The author's incisive analysis exposes the fascist underpinnings of the Muslim Brotherhood, Hamas, Hezbollah, the Shia regime in Iran, ISIS, Salafi and Jihadist ideologies, and more.  Forcefully argued and well-researched, this book grew out of a lecture on Islamic fascism that the author gave in Cairo,  resulting in a call for his death by three prominent Egyptian clerics"--  ☀►Provided by publisher. ================================= ☀► Author Notes ◄☀ " Hamed Abdel-Samad is a German-Egyptian political scientist and author of several best-selling books in Germany.  A former UNESCO consultant on Arab education, he has lectured on Islam at the University of Erfurt and Jewish and Islamic history at the University of Munich.  The son of a Sunni imam in the Egyptian city of Giza, he now ranks among Islam's fiercest critics. Educated in English and French at Cairo's Ain Shams University, politics at the German University of Augsburg, and Japanese at Kwansai Gakuin University in Japan, he has published five books, most recently the best-selling The End of the Islamic World and War or Peace: The Arab Revolution and the Future of the West .  His columns appear regularly in German national newspapers such as Die Zeit, Die Welt , and Cicero , and he appears frequently as a guest on television and radio programs across the German-speaking world." ================================= =================================
